reviews.title: NOISY NEIGHBOURHOOD!!
reviews.text:
We planned for traditional accommodations for our trip. Coming fron Liu Men (Chinese Peranakan style) to here (Malay wooden house in stilts) was a disaster. They was change of hotel management with messy check in area which is not near parking lobby. Most of the facilities photos were NOT available,  most under "renovation." Pool is not for swimming, less than 5m across.  Concierge is non existent.

The most ridiculous issue was LOUD noise. Hotel is surrounded by low cost apartments. Our villa immediate apartment played party musics until 6am!!! I complained to the guards at 12.30am. Called reception twice at 3am. No response nor even an acknowledgement during check out. Me and my missus were wide awake to the BOOM BOOM noise. I had multiple video recordings of the party, completed with disco coloured lights from the apartment. 

We are hotel guest staying 8n the so called luxury villa, and I would have prefer to sleep in my car on the road side. The duty to maintain peace for proper sleep is utmost responsibility of any hotel operator which was ignored by the hotel. I would hav checked out at 3am if not for my mother who is staying in another building of the same hotel. This is totally unacceptable.

reviews.title: Bad👎
reviews.text:
Overall, it’s a bad experience for my stay.👎🏻
My first bad review dedicated to Sojourn.
1. The check in hour is 3pm, they arrange a room to us around 2.55pm which the corridor is full of garbage. We went back to lobby & feedback around 3pm & they have explained that they arrange earlier check in, hence they not yet clean the corridor but indicated that the room is cleaned. Even u can explained you are short of manpower but u didn’t & no apologies. Keep emphasising that the room is cleaned which it’s not the key point. The explanation is juz too unreasonable. It’s a really bad impression to your customer! We request to change to another room but full house so we have to downgraded our room wif no refund.

2. Room Hygiene issue: The table in the room is dirty with food scraps, and there’s someone sunscreen left in drawer. The toilet bowl also…. 

3. It’s the first time that I faced empty shampoo & body wash bottles in hotel room, it didn’t happened for RM100 budget shop-lot hotels.
4. The receptionist say if anything can call them but no telephone in our room.
5. A glass drinking water bottle cost us RM30 which it was self breakage not dropping on ground. We should thanks that no one was hurt.

One of us stayed at this hotel before which was quite satisfying but it is really a disappointing experience for this time.
Maybe we were juz unlucky but I m sharing what we experienced.

reviews.title: Cleanliness need to be inproved
reviews.text: We received a pillow and comforter that has mould on it. The mouldy pillow was faced against the wall so we did not notice it at first. We noticed it when its already midnight bcs we wanted to sleep. Put the pillow on the floor so that tmw the housekeeping would see the mouldy pillow but when we reached the hotel again, it is still on the floor, untouched and unchanged. We called the receptionist and then, finally they changed it. So we checked the bathroom, they did not change the face towel at all. Had to call them again to change it. As for the comforter, we asked to change but was told that there will be charges. So had to bear with the mouldy comforter. In the picture, it looks like its spacious but actually its very narrow. Certain place is dusty already and not being cleaned while the toilet’s ceiling is mouldy and the blind is also dirty.

reviews.title: It's getting worst

I left the room in the morning and came back at night to see the room uncleaned. The twin bed is hard and not comfortable at all. I won't come again.

The kettle in the room smell very bad, it is very not convenient for us as we got a small baby need hot water for milk. Night time when I have time only noticed the kettle with a layer of white stain inside but baby already drink the smelly hot water in the afternoon 🫠, washed it up with the detergent I brough along to clean my baby milk bottle.

The door lock dropped, not a big issue, asking the staff help to fixed it. The ceiling of toilet looks scary. 

Kids are looking forward to the swimming pool, but was told the swimming pool can only be use after 7pm until next day before 1030am 🙃

reviews.title: Not a high standard of cleanliness
reviews.text: We stayed at this hotel for two nights in the beginning of Feb 2015. The hotel is well decorated but badly cleaned. The hotel is full of paintings and antiques. But when we checked in at 9pm, there were lots of hairs on room's floor, bed and bathroom. It looked dirty and disgusting. We asked the staff to clean it immediately. Next day we spent the whole day outside. When we went back to our room at 7pm, the room hasn't been cleaned yet! It's unacceptable! The staff told us "no enough manpower". Now you know what the problem is! Besides, the hotel is a little far from the central area of Melaka but free shuttle bus is on service; the gift from hotel- a cute notebook which is a plus. There are very few choices on breakfast. In conclusion, the Cost/Performance value is not very high so I would not recommend this hotel.

Stay for 2 days, have requested to housekeep the room from morning until we return at 5pm still nothing to be done. Called few times, still did not attend to us, manager told us several rooms also complaint no housekeeping. In the end, i go and find them, just to request for clean bath towel and mineral water. I wonder how do they claim for 5 stars service. The hotel is a small hotel yet they cant cope with the crowd. They ask us to be considerate due to festive season, so any consideration to our money paid during festive season? Is a very nice hotel but please improve service otherwise how to sustain.

reviews.title: Great hotel but poor location
reviews.text:
I booked this hotel during promotion period and we booked Vila. Room wise is great, spacious, authentic feel. Only thing was the AC spoilt within few hours of stay, the management promptly switch us to another room (however, they sent a technician to look at the AC, and took about 30 mins to verify the problem, they could have just send me to another room and the technician would get plenty of time)

Location wise, it is located outside of the city center, i drove there, and it took me 15mins to look for the entrance of the parking, and i actually have to get down of my car to ask ard for directions. There is no clear signboard of what so ever to show us how to get into the parking. Also at this location, travelers without transport will have difficulties in getting a Taxi so do keep a note on that. 

Staffs is a plus point, probably during my stay, it was a new hotel, i think i am the only customer check in

Opposite of the hotel is a ??mall or some center for performance, and they actually have performance on the day i stayed. Imagine i got a young kid with me, and they sang Kare okay until almost midnight. 

I would probably never come back to this hotel, as there are much better hotels around in melaka!!!

reviews.text:
Every inside in hotel is beautiful. 
Location far from the central Melaka. Heavy traffic around. Near to live road and spiritual loudspeakers. Best/affordable (more quantity/tasty than miser food makers in KL), lot of Chinese food corners are around. 
Breakfast arrangements are worst. Encountering houseflies. Except general breakfast items rest e.g. Malaysian food got to ask upon.
Took long time to delivered, seems like they don't want us to sit longer for breakfast. Rice was not fresh to us. My kid vomit later. Parking arrangements are unsecured.
Bed and AC are arranged diagonally. Except AC cooling adjustment (but not so) other options made unavailable. Beds were pulled to AC . 
In bathroom, kids must be careful with tap extension touching buttock and hot tap surface.  
They have no refrigerator but only non-cooling mini bar.  
Recommended for backpackers.

reviews.title: Shockingly Noisey
reviews.text:
Overall very poor because the hotel was hosting a wedding just below my window. The loud sound check began at 8:30am on a Sunday, followed by 2 hours of extremely loud speaches. The hotel was mad to organise this so early and terrible not to reduce the noise of the microphone after I complained. If the hotel had said "please not that if you stay with us on Sunday, you won't be able to sleep" then I would have reconsidered booking. It was in fact a very small wedding, did you even need such a loud mic? 

In addition to this, the breakfast was made up of all the cheapest things money could buy.

Also, it's poorly located in a town with verg few taxis.

Although the hotel looks pretty, organising a loud wedding early in the morning and not reducing the noise after I complained was extremely frustrating.

reviews.title: Biggest disappointment
reviews.text:
I’ve booked luxury villa last weekend.

This is my biggest regret for my booking in agoda so far because:
1)The floor in my villa is cracking here and there, totally not worth with the amount that I’ve paid 
2)My corridor lamp is not functioning at all,they even knocked my door for many times to repair but still cannot be switched on
3)Wardrobe button was broken, mosquito net was not functioning well
4)Few days before check in,ive  called this hotel to ask about how much the deposit and they guy told me there’s no deposit and only pay rm2 additional for tax. End up,when we about to check in, they ask for rm200 for cash deposit. If pay deposit by card,they will need three months for us to get back. This one was really pissed me off
5)For those coming there,will need to sign an agreement about the hotel policy,and there’s a policy that if we check out later than 12pm,let say 12.01 or what,u will be charged 50% of the room rate. Meanwhile check in time at 3pm. How beautiful is that.

Pls improve!

reviews.title: Was Expecting More for a 3 or 4 Star Hotel
reviews.text:
The staff were exceptional. They were very helpful, friendly and attentive.

The hotel itself is run down and urgently needs an uplift. Our room was clean and tidy.

There is a pool in front of the spa, but it is near the car park entrance- no privacy.  It is tiny, one would just dip in and jump out. We did not use it.

We did not use the Spa. We cannot comment on the Spa but it looked nice from the outside.
Beware, parking is not easy to find . We had problems because the sign was blocked by parked cars. The helpful staff came out of the hotel to help us!

We enjoyed the breakfast in a pretty area. The staff who took the orders was polite and all smiles.

TSH staff made it enjoyable to stay there.
reviews.response: Thank you for stayed with us and hope to welcoming you again near future.

reviews.title: Could be better
reviews.text:
The Settlement Hotel is a reasonably priced hotel in a good location, but the rooms are smaller than an average hotel room. If you are travelling with more than 2 people, there won't be enough space in the room.

The Hotel prides itself on the service of its staff, and I notice they seem to permanently take in attachment students from a culinary school. I would agree to a certain extent that their service were satisfactory. However, there are definitely room for improvement. At around 10.30pm, I went down to the front desk to request to purchase a slice of cheese cake, which was displayed at the counter behind the front desk. The staff told me that they only sold up till 10pm and declined me. When I enquired why the cakes were still on display if so, he called the kitchen staff to come down. Over the walkie talkie, I could hear the kitchen staff saying "malaslah" ("I'm lazy"). After a few minutes, a kitchen staff came to apologize that they could not sell me the cake because "the payment counter is closed". I suggested that I just gave him the money (I have the exact amount) for him to give the "payment counter" the next morning. The kitchen staff declined my request. When I said I could serve myself and pay him the money (since the display could be opened easily), the staff told me the cakes were not edible and "on display only". That sounded odd to me because the cakes look perfectly fine, but I didn't insist further.

After I went out to buy some food, I went back to the hotel and passed the front desk. I saw that they have removed all the cakes and even the next day when I checked out, the cake displays were empty. This was very odd, especially since there were signs around the hotel and fliers in the hotel room promoting their cakes and encouraging guests to buy them!

reviews.title: Alright for one night, interesting choice of decor
reviews.text:
We had problems checking in at first due to a glitch between Agoda's reservation and the hotel. Hotel manager was rather rude and atributed it to a declined credit card! After showing the proof of payment and voucher from Agoda and even having to go to the extent of making an international call to Agoda, it was sorted out between the hotel and agoda that it was a mistake. The hotel manager did not have the courtesy to apologize after for his mistake. 

We were then told they did not have the room we had booked and made to wait an additional half hour for it. What made up for this was the fact that it happened to be the first day of the chinese new year and it appeared as though the family of/and/or the owners of the hotel were present. They apologized for the mistake and we received rather good hospitality before the check-in.

The room decor was quite something i must say, nice water bottles, victorian-like tea-set, balinese bathroom & tub, clean and new. It was a mix of cultures all in one room but modern at the same time. I only hope it remains this way and doesnt dwindle in time. Only downside were the terribly thin walls between rooms where we could literally hear what was going on next door and across the hall! A small hotel and expected. And that the hotel is actually located next to a sewer and where parking is a torture.

TIP: Staff should probably not take on the use of foreign accents that are hard to comprehend by Asians.

Stay here if you are looking to tour the portugese settlement. Else, choose a central hotel.

1. The service - please train those young foreign men (or shall I say boys) as they don't know what hospitality  is what more on service, etiquette and decorum. I know the hotel is under a new management now but a hotel without good staff and service means nothing.  Do train your staff, Herbaline - and start with understanding basic English or Malay as few of them don't even understand or speak any of those languages.
2. Cleanliness - we stayed in a Villa and obviously we expected a spotless cleanliness depicting the name 'Villa'. The table and remote control are sticky and the toilet had some dropping. When we alerted the front office, the cleaner came with a broom, yes, a broom, period...

However, it was still worth the money but I won't stay here again.

reviews.title: Old and not well maintained
reviews.text:
We requested in advance for a room at a higher floor but was arranged for a room at level 1. Upon checking-in, we realise that the room doesn't have a safety lock (the chain was broken) and hence went back down with our luggage to request for a change of room due to security concerns. We also mentioned to the front desk staff on our previous request through Agoda whereby management replied approved but we weren't given a higher level room. Staff quickly rearranged a room at level 2 for us but looked impatient/displeased.

Upon checking-in to the new room, we noticed that this room too had the same issue - this time the safety lock doesn't even have the chain on it anymore. Thankfully we were prepared and brought our own safety device (door lock) hence to avoid the hassle of having bring our luggage down once more (and it seemed like they didn't have much room left), we remained in this room.

Overall the room is clean but the maintenance of the room needs to be improved, safety lock should be installed for each room and the lock on the bathroom door was also dangling and can't be used. Also, it would be helpful for Sojourn to let their customers know that the Herbaline spa facilities are not managed by the guest house so that customers can arrange in advance with Herbaline directly to avoid disappointments.

reviews.title: Need Maintenance
reviews.text:
The spacious room and cleanliness were definite highlights of our stay, and the location was convenient. 

However, the experience was marred by several maintenance issues. Despite the aesthetic appeal of the bathtub, the reality was disappointing. The sink, bidet and bathtub had leaking pipes, rendering them unusable for their intended purposes. Despite notifying the staff, the attempted fix proved ineffective, as the leaking persisted, preventing us from enjoying a proper bath experience.

Overall, while the potential for relaxation was there, the lack of functional amenities due to maintenance issues was a significant drawback.

* I had room 207 (next to the linen room, not to noisy. For those planning to book here, make sure you check the room once checked in to avoid disappointment)

reviews.title: boutique hotel near Portuguese settlement
reviews.text:
On 1st day the lunch was a disaster but the recovery meals by the chef was encouraging. However a Christmas dinner that was not traditional for us i.e. seafood  was a gamble after the 1st day tragedy.  
Taking the gamble as we were tried of going out; dinner was served at 1930hrs and to my surprise it was good. The environment had been good with Christmas music and relaxing feeling. 
The kids were just happy to relax by the pool or play games in the rooms. The pool was small but enough to wade in as my boys enjoyed swimming. Walking around the hotel had a few good spots for posing for photos. 
The area around the hotel with flats was a bit of an eye  sore but what to do. For Christmas day we went out to town to get some shopping in jonker street. Came back to the hotel to relax. On a average out of 10 being the best the hotel, I would say a 7 for the service of the staff. Front office manager Lim and chef Amirul get the best efforts to try to compensate for the services. 
However the hotel needs to train on language and communications skills for the front liners.
We had a pleasant stay and enjoy some peace and quiet times with family
reviews.positives: The rooms was an average size with good features and size. The location nearby had some benefits of just walking distance to the Portuguese settlement where the true meaning of Christmas decorations.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
Had a rather comfortable stay with my husband but there are few issues that me & my husband agreed on :

1. The room that i booked is Junior King Suite which i expected to be looking a little luxurious but the room is dull, also got chipped paint on the wall. Honestly, i felt that what we got is not what we paid for.

2. The only luxurious-looking thing in the room is the bathroom. 

3. Both of us LOVE the herbal scent we smelt while staying there.

4. Requested honeymoon deco & late check out at 1pm and the property agreed to provide swan towel & also late check out at 1pm is available. Upon arriving, the staff informed check out is at 11am and i asked about the late check out. I was informed the latest check out is 12pm which won’t be charge and checkout any later than that will be charge which is understandable. At 11.40am, a staff called to inform she already charge for late checkout which i do not understand why. But after my husband explain we already inform about the late checout during checking in, then only she said okay. I hope the staffs learn to take note of guest’s request to avoid this kind of things from happening. I also did not get the swan towel that i requested.

5. I seriously do not like the parking provided. At the front, only 3-4 cars can fit. If it’s full, you have to go to the back of the property that have uneven road and don’t have a proper gate. Got wired fence but it’s all cut down. I felt less secure when i parked there. But at night, we manage to park in front as there’s an empty spot available. 

6. Called for housekeeping as our aircond is not cold even after a while. Minutes later, a guy came and turns out only need to fix the settings. I am thankful for his fast response to act upon guest’s complains. 

Overall, i only had slight issues with the facilities and room. The staffs are helpful, polite and pleasant. But i hope next time the staff learn to take note of  guest’s request and the agreed request can be prepared such as the honeymoon deco and late checkout.

Overall, the hotel is comfortable and cozy.

reviews.title: Comfortable & Spacious Room
reviews.text: A very humble looking exterior in the midst of old bungalows, but once you step inside The Settlement Hotel, you are greeted and welcomed with immaculate peranakan decor and a very well designed hotel logo with 2 mouse deer . The deluxe room we stayed in was spacious, with a large terrazzo bath tub good for 2. The room is beautifully furnished. The beddings and pillows were very comfortable. The room can accommodate a King Size bed but we had to settle for a Queen! Special mention is the soft bath towels, with a simple logo, which was a delight to use! The buffet breakfast (which came with the room) has a simple but reasonable spread of local and continental fares. For fruits, only watermelon was offered. Wish there were more varieties. For dessert, only 2 kind of miniature cupcake (quite dry) were offered. It would have been delectable if local peranakan nonya kueh were offered instead!

- whilst not big, there are places to sit and relax in
- soft pillows, plush bedding and beautiful bathroom
- good breakfast spread although come early if you want to get the fruits and the waffles!

The Areas for Improvement
- twin beds were kids size. We had a hard time fitting in, which meant we both had poor sleep. And we are not tall by any stretch of the imagination!
- hotel is not near the city centre and whilst the hotel does provide one way complimentary shuttle, it doesn't make sense not to offer a complimentary return service since the shuttle has to come back to the hotel anyway! Anyway, it is probably best to arrange transport back from the hotel. We always ended up paying more than what it costs to get into town.
- no porters so you have to carry the bags yourself
- unlike some other people, we did not get any red velvet or cheese cake so service does not seem to be consistent.

I have stayed in a couple of other properties in Malaysia on this trip and comparatively, this was the best in terms of decor, ambience, service and uniqueness. I'd probably come back.

reviews.title: Not convenient for Muslim people
reviews.text:
The hotel itself is nice but there are several things for Muslim to consider and if they didn't mind, it is up to them.

1. The hotel is not Muslim friendly or convenient. 
a. The hotel restaurant didn't have Halal certificate although it is no pork meals. 
b. No signage for Qiblat. There are lots of hotel with that signage. And even provide sejadah though the owner is non-muslim.
c. Location: not so easy to find halal restaurant nearby.

2. It has the best bathtub I ever been to any hotel. It is spacious. The water pressure is high. So satisfying for relaxation.
a. But there is no comb provided. Only toothbrush, shower cap, the basics. But usually other hotel will include comb. They should include this for next time if they want improvement.
b. The shampoo, conditioner, shower gel is Super tiptop. Smells really nice and they gave a lot. Super thumbs up. 
c. Unfortunately, the iron and the iron board is not well maintained. Worn out. Not useful enough.
d. The bed... The bedframe on one site already sagged? Not a quality bed frame given unfortunately. The pillow also need improvement. And even the mattress.
e. The room itself is beautiful but should consider carpet. Too cold. My type is not spacious but very comfortable and cozy room.
3. The staffs
Really nice people. Really good service.
4. Check out time
Received call and reminded for check out at 11.30 a.m which is different from other hotel I have been with. Usually check out time is 12.00pm. but before 11.30 the already called to remind to check out and even the room service already waited outside our room. Need to rush packing our belongings, really make turned off the experience or considering repeating to sleep here again. Why 11.30 am? ... Not very customer friendly rules. 
5. Swimming pool- very small, kinda disappointed.

Overall, the hotel is pricey but considering the bathtub (haha), maybe if I want to repeat just for the pleasure of relaxation only.  The location, the facilities for me not very satisfying.

reviews.title: Beautiful Oriental - Portuguese style boutique hotel
reviews.text:
The winner of the best 4-star hotel in Malaysia (2016-17), this hotel has smartness and style which pushes it beyond the norm for Malaysian hotels. Beautiful and exceptional design if you're a historical lover.
Coming in at a reasonable price, the rooms are classically styled, with neat detailing and excellent bathrooms. The stone bathtubs are a great addition and offer long comfortable soaks. The beds are excellent.
The reception service was average - foreigner staff who unable to give clear information and leads to miscommunication. However they are friendly and pleasant.
Overall - this is a great place to sleep and use as a base while in Malacca, but don't expect too much service.

reviews.text: This is my 4th time staying in this hotel. I would rate my staying as an overall satisfactory not really great due to certain things such as the property didn’t message us earlier that the road has been changed due to some road renovation, last time it was so close to the main road now we had to take a little detour to park our car. When we check in, the hand bidet for toilet was broke but the staff immediately changed it, I appreciate their efficiency. However, the floor was a bit dusty. I love this place whenever i come to melaka i always choose this place so i think it’s time for some upgrading, refurbish few things bc this place starts to become more and more popular.

reviews.title: Comfortable
reviews.positives: The hotel was clean. Fridge, hairdryer, coffee machine were available in the room. With the price point is totally worth it.
reviews.negatives: The spring bed is quite old and outworn already. I hope management can made changes about this part and make the hotel experience become excellent rather than good.
